---
title: "Casual Inference Final Project"
author: "Safia Muftic, Olivia Varricchione, and Surbhi Nahata"
date: "2026-03-26"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  message = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE
)

# load tidyverse package 
library(tidyverse)
# load haven package 
library(haven) 
# load dplyr package 
library(dplyr)
# load tidyr package 
library(tidyr) 
# load broom package 
library(broom)  
# load did package for  difference-in-differences analysis (Callaway and Sant’Anna)
library(did)
# load gtsummary package
library(gtsummary)
# load tidycensus
library(tidycensus)
```

```{r 2017 Prep}
# file path to 2017 zip file
zip_path1 <- "LLCP2017XPT.zip"
# Create a temporary folder to extract the XPT
tmp <- tempdir()
# Extract the XPT file from the ZIP into the temporary folder
xpt_file <- unzip(zip_path1, files = "LLCP2017.XPT ", exdir = tmp)
# Read the SAS transport file into R
brfss2017 <- read_xpt(xpt_file)
#Select variables of interest
brfss2017 <- brfss2017 |> select(MENTHLTH, SEX, `_RACE`, INCOME2, EMPLOY1, GENHLTH, `_AGEG5YR`, `_STATE`)
#Add year variable
brfss2017 <- brfss2017 |> mutate(Year = 2017)
# View the first few rows of the dataset to understand the structure
head(brfss2017)
```

```{r 2018 Prep}
# file path to 2018 zip file
zip_path2 <- "LLCP2018XPT.zip"
# Create a temporary folder to extract the XPT
tmp <- tempdir()
# Extract the XPT file from the ZIP into the temporary folder
xpt_file <- unzip(zip_path2, files = "LLCP2018.XPT ", exdir = tmp)
# Read the SAS transport file into R
brfss2018 <- read_xpt(xpt_file)
# select variables
brfss2018 <- brfss2018 |> select(MENTHLTH, SEX1, `_RACE`, INCOME2, EMPLOY1, GENHLTH, `_AGEG5YR`, `_STATE`)
#To keep consistency for merging, change SEX1 to SEX to match 2017
brfss2018 <- rename(brfss2018, SEX = SEX1)
#Add year variable
brfss2018 <- brfss2018 |> mutate(Year = 2018)
# View the first few rows of the dataset to understand the structure
head(brfss2018)
```

```{r 2019 Prep}
# file path to 2019 zip file
zip_path3 <- "LLCP2019XPT.zip"
# Create a temporary folder to extract the XPT
tmp <- tempdir()
# Extract the XPT file from the ZIP into the temporary folder
xpt_file <- unzip(zip_path3, files = "LLCP2019.XPT ", exdir = tmp)
# Read the SAS transport file into R
brfss2019 <- read_xpt(xpt_file)
# select variables
brfss2019 <- brfss2019 |> select(MENTHLTH, SEXVAR, `_RACE`, INCOME2, EMPLOY1, GENHLTH, `_AGEG5YR`, `_STATE`)
#To keep consistency for merging, change SEXVAR to SEX to match 2017 and 2018
brfss2019 <- rename(brfss2019, SEX = SEXVAR)
#Add year variable
brfss2019 <- brfss2019 |> mutate(Year = 2019)
# View the first few rows of the dataset to understand the structure
head(brfss2019)
```

```{r 2020 Prep}
# file path to 2020 zip file
zip_path4 <- "LLCP2020XPT.zip"
# Create a temporary folder to extract the XPT
tmp <- tempdir()
# Extract the XPT file from the ZIP into the temporary folder
xpt_file <- unzip(zip_path4, files = "LLCP2020.XPT ", exdir = tmp)
# Read the SAS transport file into R
brfss2020 <- read_xpt(xpt_file)
# select variables
brfss2020 <- brfss2020 |> select(MENTHLTH, SEXVAR, `_RACE`, INCOME2, EMPLOY1, GENHLTH, `_AGEG5YR`, `_STATE`)
#To keep consistency for merging, change SEXVAR to SEX to match 2017 and 2018
brfss2020 <- rename(brfss2020, SEX = SEXVAR)
#Add year variable
brfss2020 <- brfss2020 |> mutate(Year = 2020)
# View the first few rows of the dataset to understand the structure
head(brfss2020)
```

```{r 2021 Prep}
# file path to 2021 zip file
zip_path5 <- "LLCP2021XPT.zip"
# Create a temporary folder to extract the XPT
tmp <- tempdir()
# Extract the XPT file from the ZIP into the temporary folder
xpt_file <- unzip(zip_path5
                  , files = "LLCP2021.XPT ", exdir = tmp)
# Read the SAS transport file into R
brfss2021 <- read_xpt(xpt_file)
# select variables
brfss2021 <- brfss2021 |> select(MENTHLTH, SEXVAR, `_RACE`, INCOME3, EMPLOY1, GENHLTH, `_AGEG5YR`, `_STATE`)
#To keep consistency for merging, change SEXVAR to SEX to match 2017 and 2018
brfss2021 <- rename(brfss2021, SEX = SEXVAR)
#Also, recode INCOME3 to be consistent with INCOME2
#8 = $75,000 or more in INCOME2, need to change levels 7, 8, 9, 10, and 11 to be $75,000 or more
brfss2021 <- brfss2021 |> mutate(INCOME3 = as.factor(INCOME3)) |>
  mutate(INCOME3 = case_when(INCOME3 %in% c("7", "8", "9", "10", "11") ~ "7",
                                        .default = INCOME3)) |>
  mutate(INCOME2 = as.double(INCOME3)) |>
  select(!INCOME3)
#Add year variable
brfss2021 <- brfss2021 |> mutate(Year = 2021)
# View the first few rows of the dataset to understand the structure
head(brfss2021)
```

```{r 2022 Prep}
# file path to 2022 zip file
zip_path6 <- "LLCP2022XPT.zip"
# Create a temporary folder to extract the XPT
tmp <- tempdir()
# Extract the XPT file from the ZIP into the temporary folder
xpt_file <- unzip(zip_path6, files = "LLCP2022.XPT ", exdir = tmp)
# Read the SAS transport file into R
brfss2022 <- read_xpt(xpt_file)
# select variables
brfss2022 <- brfss2022 |> select(MENTHLTH, SEXVAR, `_RACE1`, INCOME3, EMPLOY1, GENHLTH, `_AGEG5YR`, `_STATE`)
#To keep consistency for merging, change SEXVAR to SEX to match 2017 and 2018
brfss2022 <- rename(brfss2022, SEX = SEXVAR)
#Also change _RACE1 to _RACE
brfss2022 <- rename(brfss2022, `_RACE` = `_RACE1`)
#Also change INCOME3 to match INCOME2
brfss2022 <- brfss2022 |> mutate(INCOME3 = as.factor(INCOME3)) |>
  mutate(INCOME3 = case_when(INCOME3 %in% c("7", "8", "9", "10", "11") ~ "7",
                                        .default = INCOME3)) |>
  mutate(INCOME2 = as.double(INCOME3)) |>
  select(!INCOME3)
#Add year variable
brfss2022 <- brfss2022 |> mutate(Year = 2022)
# View the first few rows of the dataset to understand the structure
head(brfss2022)
```

```{r Merge}
# Combine all years into one dataset
df <- bind_rows(brfss2017, brfss2018, brfss2019, brfss2020, brfss2021, brfss2022)
```

```{r Relabel as needed}
# recode mental health variable (88 = 0 days, 77/99 = missing)
df <- df |>
  mutate(MENTHLTH = case_when(
    MENTHLTH == 88 ~ 0,
    MENTHLTH == 77 ~ NA_real_,
    MENTHLTH == 99 ~ NA_real_,
    TRUE ~ as.numeric(MENTHLTH)
  ))

# recode sex into labeled factor
df <- df |> mutate(SEX = case_when(SEX == 1 ~ "Male",
                         SEX == 2 ~ "Female")) |>
  mutate(SEX = as.factor(SEX))

# recode race into categories
df <- df |> mutate(`_RACE` = case_when(`_RACE` == 1 ~ "White",
                           `_RACE` == 2 ~ "Black",
                           `_RACE` == 3 ~ "American Indian/Alaskan Native",
                           `_RACE` == 4 ~ "Asian",
                           `_RACE` == 5 ~ "Native Hawaiian/Pacific Islander",
                           `_RACE` == 6 ~ "Other",
                           `_RACE` == 7 ~ "Multiracial",
                           `_RACE` == 8 ~ "Hispanic")) |>
  mutate(`_RACE` = as.factor(`_RACE`))

# recode income into categories
df <- df |> mutate(INCOME2 = as.factor(INCOME2)) |>
  mutate(INCOME2 = case_when(INCOME2 == 1 ~ "<$10,000",
                             INCOME2 == 2 ~ "$10,000-$15,000",
                             INCOME2 == 3 ~ "$15,000-$20,000",
                             INCOME2 == 4 ~ "$20,000-$25,000",
                             INCOME2 == 5 ~ "$25,000-$35,000",
                             INCOME2 == 6 ~ "$35,000-$50,000",
                             INCOME2 == 7 ~ "$50,000-$75,000",
                             INCOME2 == 8 ~ "$75,000+")) |>
  mutate(INCOME2 = as.factor(INCOME2))

# recode employment status
df <- df |> mutate(EMPLOY1 = as.factor(EMPLOY1)) |>
  mutate(EMPLOY1 = case_when(EMPLOY1 == 1 ~ "Employed",
                             EMPLOY1 == 2 ~ "Self-employed",
                             EMPLOY1 == 3 ~ "Out of work 1+ years",
                             EMPLOY1 == 4 ~ "Out of work <1 year",
                             EMPLOY1 == 5 ~ "Homemaker",
                             EMPLOY1 == 6 ~ "Student",
                             EMPLOY1 == 7 ~ "Retired",
                             EMPLOY1 == 8 ~ "Unable to work")) |>
  mutate(EMPLOY1 = as.factor(EMPLOY1))


# recode age groups
df <- df |> mutate(GENHLTH = as.factor(GENHLTH)) |>
  mutate(GENHLTH = case_when(GENHLTH == 1 ~ "Excellent",
                             GENHLTH == 2 ~ "Very good",
                             GENHLTH == 3 ~ "Good",
                             GENHLTH == 4 ~ "Fair",
                             GENHLTH == 5 ~ "Poor")) |>
   mutate(GENHLTH = as.factor(GENHLTH))

# recode age groups
df <- df |> mutate(`_AGEG5YR` = as.factor(`_AGEG5YR`)) |>
  mutate(`_AGEG5YR` = case_when(`_AGEG5YR` == 1 ~ "18-24",
                                `_AGEG5YR` == 2 ~ "25-29",
                                `_AGEG5YR` == 3 ~ "30-34",
                                `_AGEG5YR` == 4 ~ "35-39",
                                `_AGEG5YR` == 5 ~ "40-44",
                                `_AGEG5YR` == 6 ~ "45-49",
                                `_AGEG5YR` == 7 ~ "50-54",
                                `_AGEG5YR` == 8 ~ "55-59",
                                `_AGEG5YR` == 9 ~ "60-64",
                                `_AGEG5YR` == 10 ~ "65-69",
                                `_AGEG5YR` == 11 ~ "70-74",
                                `_AGEG5YR` == 12 ~ "75-79",
                                `_AGEG5YR` == 13 ~ "80+")) |>
  mutate(`_AGEG5YR` = as.factor(`_AGEG5YR`))


# recode state names
df <- df |> mutate(`_STATE` = case_when(`_STATE` == 1 ~ "Alabama",
                              `_STATE` == 2 ~ "Alaska",
                              `_STATE` == 4 ~ "Arizona",
                              `_STATE` == 5 ~ "Arkansas",
                              `_STATE` == 6 ~ "California",
                              `_STATE` == 8 ~ "Colorado",
                              `_STATE` == 9 ~ "Connecticut",
                              `_STATE` == 10 ~ "Delaware",
                              `_STATE` == 11 ~ "District of Columbia",
                              `_STATE` == 12 ~ "Florida",
                              `_STATE` == 13 ~ "Georgia",
                              `_STATE` == 15 ~ "Hawaii",
                              `_STATE` == 16 ~ "Idaho",
                              `_STATE` == 17 ~ "Illinois",
                              `_STATE` == 18 ~ "Indiana",
                              `_STATE` == 19 ~ "Iowa",
                              `_STATE` == 20 ~ "Kansas",
                              `_STATE` == 21 ~ "Kentucky",
                              `_STATE` == 22 ~ "Louisiana",
                              `_STATE` == 23 ~ "Maine",
                              `_STATE` == 24 ~ "Maryland",
                              `_STATE` == 25 ~ "Massachusetts",
                              `_STATE` == 26 ~ "Michigan",
                              `_STATE` == 27 ~ "Minnesota",
                              `_STATE` == 28 ~ "Mississippi",
                              `_STATE` == 29 ~ "Missouri",
                              `_STATE` == 30 ~ "Montana",
                              `_STATE` == 31 ~ "Nebraska",
                              `_STATE` == 32 ~ "Nevada",
                              `_STATE` == 33 ~ "New Hampshire",
                              `_STATE` == 34 ~ "New Jersey",
                              `_STATE` == 35 ~ "New Mexico",
                              `_STATE` == 36 ~ "New York",
                              `_STATE` == 37 ~ "North Carolina",
                              `_STATE` == 38 ~ "North Dakota",
                              `_STATE` == 39 ~ "Ohio",
                              `_STATE` == 40 ~ "Oklahoma",
                              `_STATE` == 41 ~ "Oregon",
                              `_STATE` == 42 ~ "Pennsylvania",
                              `_STATE` == 44 ~ "Rhode Island",
                              `_STATE` == 45 ~ "South Carolina",
                              `_STATE` == 46 ~ "South Dakota",
                              `_STATE` == 47 ~ "Tennessee",
                              `_STATE` == 48 ~ "Texas",
                              `_STATE` == 49 ~ "Utah",
                              `_STATE` == 50 ~ "Vermont",
                              `_STATE` == 51 ~ "Virginia",
                              `_STATE` == 53 ~ "Washington",
                              `_STATE` == 54 ~ "West Virginia",
                              `_STATE` == 55 ~ "Wisconsin",
                              `_STATE` == 56 ~ "Wyoming",
                              `_STATE` == 66 ~ "Guam",
                              `_STATE` == 72 ~ "Puerto Rico")) |>
  mutate(`_STATE` = as.factor(`_STATE`))

# convert year to factor
df <- df |> mutate(Year = as.factor(Year))

# Make sure all variables are stored correctly
summary(df)

# get total number of observations in the original dataset
n_original <- nrow(df)

n_original
```

```{r Data Structure}
# preview the first few rows of the dataset to understand its structure
head(df)

# count number of observations by year to see how the data is distributed
df |> summarize(.by = Year, count = n())
```

```{r Hand Annotate Political Leaning}
# create a dataset with state political leaning (based on 2020 election)
political_data <- data.frame(
  # list of U.S. states
  state = c("Alabama", "Alaska", "Arizona", "Arkansas", "California",
                 "Colorado", "Connecticut", "Delaware", "Florida", "Georgia",
                 "Hawaii", "Idaho", "Illinois", "Indiana", "Iowa", "Kansas",
                 "Kentucky", "Louisiana", "Maine", "Maryland", "Massachusetts",
                 "Michigan", "Minnesota", "Mississippi", "Missouri", "Montana",
                 "Nebraska", "Nevada", "New Hampshire", "New Jersey",
                 "New Mexico", "New York", "North Carolina", "North Dakota",
                 "Ohio", "Oklahoma", "Oregon", "Pennsylvania", "Rhode Island",
                 "South Carolina", "South Dakota", "Tennessee", "Texas",
                 "Utah", "Vermont", "Virginia", "Washington", "Washington DC",
                 "West Virginia", "Wisconsin", "Wyoming"),
  # assign political party based on 2020 election results
  political_leaning = c(
    "Republican", "Republican", "Democrat", "Republican", "Democrat",
    "Democrat", "Democrat", "Democrat", "Republican", "Democrat",
    "Democrat", "Republican", "Democrat", "Republican", "Republican",
    "Republican", "Republican", "Republican", "Democrat", "Democrat",
    "Democrat", "Democrat", "Democrat", "Republican", "Republican",
    "Republican", "Republican", "Democrat", "Democrat", "Democrat",
    "Democrat", "Democrat", "Republican", "Republican", "Republican",
    "Republican", "Democrat", "Democrat", "Democrat", "Republican",
    "Republican", "Republican", "Republican", "Republican", "Democrat",
    "Democrat", "Democrat", "Democrat", "Republican", "Democrat",
    "Republican"
  ))


# rename Washington DC to match naming used in the main dataset
political_data$state[political_data$state == "Washington DC"] <- "District of Columbia"


# merge political leaning into main dataset by matching state names
df <- df |> left_join(political_data, by = c(`_STATE` = "state"))

# check the structure of the dataset to confirm the new variable was added
glimpse(df)
```

```{r Hand Annotate COVID Policy Strictness}
covid <- read.csv("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OxCGRT/covid-policy-dataset/main/data/OxCGRT_compact_subnational_v1.csv")
#Only USA
covid <- covid |> filter(CountryName == "United States")
#Only state-specific
covid <- covid |> filter(RegionName %in% c("Alabama", "Alaska", "Arizona", "Arkansas",
                                  "California", "Colorado", "Connecticut", "Delaware",
                                  "Florida", "Georgia", "Hawaii", "Idaho", "Illinois",
                                  "Indiana", "Iowa", "Kansas", "Kentucky", "Louisiana",
                                  "Maine", "Maryland", "Massachusetts", "Michigan",
                                  "Minnesota", "Mississippi", "Missouri", "Montana",
                                  "Nebraska", "Nevada", "New Hampshire", "New Jersey",
                                  "New Mexico", "New York", "North Carolina", 
                                  "North Dakota", "Ohio", "Oklahoma", "Oregon",
                                  "Pennsylvania", "Rhode Island", "South Carolina",
                                  "South Dakota", "Tennessee", "Texas", "Utah",
                                  "Vermont", "Virginia", "Washington", "Washington DC",
                                  "West Virginia", "Wisconsin", "Wyoming"))
#Get average stringency per year per state
annual <- covid |> mutate(year = as.numeric(substr(as.character(Date), 1, 4))) |>
  filter(year %in% c(2020, 2021, 2022)) |>
  group_by(RegionName) |>
  summarize(avg_stringency = mean(StringencyIndex_Average)) |>
  rename(state = RegionName) |>
  mutate(state = ifelse(state == "Washington DC", "District of Columbia", state)) |>
  mutate(state = as.factor(state))

#Main method
med <- median(annual$avg_stringency)
annual <- annual |> mutate(main_treatment = ifelse(avg_stringency > med, "Strict",
                                                   "Not Strict"))
#Alternative #1
q25 <- quantile(annual$avg_stringency, 0.25)
annual <- annual |> mutate(alt_treatment1 = ifelse(avg_stringency > q25, "Strict",
                                                   "Not Strict"))

#Alternative #2
q75 <- quantile(annual$avg_stringency, 0.75)
annual <- annual |> mutate(alt_treatment2 = ifelse(avg_stringency > q75, "Strict",
                                                   "Not Strict"))

#Select just the classification variables
annual <- annual |> dplyr::select(!avg_stringency)

#Add these values to the data set:
df <- df |> left_join(annual, by = c(`_STATE` = "state"))
```

```{r severity data}
pop_data <- get_estimates(geography = "state", product = "population", year = 2020) |>
  filter(variable == "POPESTIMATE") |>
  filter(NAME != "Puerto Rico") |>
  select(NAME, value)

cases <- covid |> mutate(year = as.numeric(substr(as.character(Date), 1, 4))) |>
  filter(year %in% c(2020, 2021, 2022)) |>
  group_by(RegionName) |>
  summarize(total_deaths = max(ConfirmedDeaths, na.rm = TRUE)) |>
  rename(state = RegionName) |>
  mutate(state = ifelse(state == "Washington DC", "District of Columbia", state))

cases <- cases |> left_join(pop_data, by = c("state" = "NAME")) |>
  mutate(deaths_per_100k = (total_deaths / value) * 100000) |>
  select(state, deaths_per_100k)

df <- df |> left_join(cases, by = c(`_STATE` = "state"))
```

```{r Missingness Summary}
#First, remove Guam and Puerto Rico, which are missing in the hand annotations
df <- df |> filter(`_STATE` != "Guam" & `_STATE` != "Puerto Rico")

# calculate number and percentage of missing values for each variable
df |> 
  summarise(across(everything(), 
                   list(
                     missing_n = ~sum(is.na(.)),
                     missing_pct = ~mean(is.na(.)) * 100
                   ))) |> 
  pivot_longer(
    cols = everything(),
    names_to = c("variable", ".value"),
    names_sep = "_missing_"
  ) |> 
  mutate(pct = round(pct, 2)) |> 
  arrange(desc(pct))
```


```{r Missing Data Patterns}
# Check whether missing income is related to other variables
result <- df %>%
  mutate(missing_income = is.na(INCOME2)) %>%
  group_by(missing_income) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean_menthlth = mean(MENTHLTH, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_deaths = mean(deaths_per_100k, na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_male = mean(SEX == "Male", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_female = mean(SEX == "Female", na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_age_group = mean(as.numeric(`_AGEG5YR`), na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_year=mean(as.numeric(Year), na.rm=TRUE),
    mean_strict_main=mean(main_treatment=="Strict", na.rm=TRUE),
    mean_not_strict_main=mean(main_treatment=="Not Strict", na.rm=TRUE),
    prop_alt1_strict = mean(alt_treatment1 == "Strict", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_alt1_not_strict = mean(alt_treatment1 == "Not Strict", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_alt2_strict = mean(alt_treatment2 == "Strict", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_alt2_not_strict = mean(alt_treatment2 == "Not Strict", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_excellent = mean(GENHLTH == "Excellent", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_verygood = mean(GENHLTH == "Very good", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_good = mean(GENHLTH == "Good", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_fair = mean(GENHLTH == "Fair", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_poor = mean(GENHLTH == "Poor", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_white = mean(`_RACE` == "White", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_black = mean(`_RACE` == "Black", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_hispanic = mean(`_RACE` == "Hispanic", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_asian = mean(`_RACE` == "Asian", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_multiracial = mean(`_RACE` == "Multiracial", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_race_other = mean(`_RACE` == "Other", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_employed=mean(EMPLOY1 == "Employed", na.rm=TRUE),
    prop_retired=mean(EMPLOY1 == "Retired", na.rm=TRUE),
    prop_self_employed=mean(EMPLOY1 == "Self-employed", na.rm=TRUE),
    prop_unable=mean(EMPLOY1 == "Unable to work", na.rm=TRUE),
    prop_homemaker=mean(EMPLOY1 == "Homemaker", na.rm=TRUE),
    prop_student=mean(EMPLOY1 == "Student", na.rm=TRUE),
    prop_democrat=mean(political_leaning == "Democrat", na.rm=TRUE),
    prop_republican=mean(political_leaning == "Republican", na.rm=TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

print(result, width = Inf)

# Check whether missing mental health is related to other variables
result2 <- df %>%
  mutate(missing_menthlth = is.na(MENTHLTH)) %>%
  group_by(missing_menthlth) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean_income = mean(as.numeric(INCOME2), na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_deaths = mean(deaths_per_100k, na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_male = mean(SEX == "Male", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_female = mean(SEX == "Female", na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_age_group = mean(as.numeric(`_AGEG5YR`), na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_year=mean(as.numeric(Year), na.rm=TRUE),
    mean_strict=mean(main_treatment=="Strict", na.rm=TRUE),
    mean_not_strict=mean(main_treatment=="Not Strict", na.rm=TRUE),
    prop_alt1_strict = mean(alt_treatment1 == "Strict", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_alt1_not_strict = mean(alt_treatment1 == "Not Strict", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_alt2_strict = mean(alt_treatment2 == "Strict", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_alt2_not_strict = mean(alt_treatment2 == "Not Strict", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_excellent = mean(GENHLTH == "Excellent", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_verygood = mean(GENHLTH == "Very good", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_good = mean(GENHLTH == "Good", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_fair = mean(GENHLTH == "Fair", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_white = mean(`_RACE` == "White", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_black = mean(`_RACE` == "Black", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_hispanic = mean(`_RACE` == "Hispanic", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_asian = mean(`_RACE` == "Asian", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_multiracial = mean(`_RACE` == "Multiracial", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_race_other = mean(`_RACE` == "Other", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_employed=mean(EMPLOY1 == "Employed", na.rm=TRUE),
    prop_retired=mean(EMPLOY1 == "Retired", na.rm=TRUE),
    prop_self_employed=mean(EMPLOY1 == "Self-employed", na.rm=TRUE),
    prop_unable=mean(EMPLOY1 == "Unable to work", na.rm=TRUE),
    prop_homemaker=mean(EMPLOY1 == "Homemaker", na.rm=TRUE),
    prop_student=mean(EMPLOY1 == "Student", na.rm=TRUE),
    prop_democrat=mean(political_leaning == "Democrat", na.rm=TRUE),
    prop_republican=mean(political_leaning == "Republican", na.rm=TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

print(result2, width = Inf)

# Check whether missing race is related to other variables
result4 <- df %>%
  mutate(missing_race = is.na(`_RACE`)) %>%
  group_by(missing_race) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean_income = mean(as.numeric(INCOME2), na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_deaths = mean(deaths_per_100k, na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_male = mean(SEX == "Male", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_female = mean(SEX == "Female", na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_age_group = mean(as.numeric(`_AGEG5YR`), na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_year=mean(as.numeric(Year), na.rm=TRUE),
    mean_strict=mean(main_treatment=="Strict", na.rm=TRUE),
    mean_not_strict=mean(main_treatment=="Not Strict", na.rm=TRUE),
    prop_excellent = mean(GENHLTH == "Excellent", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_verygood = mean(GENHLTH == "Very good", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_good = mean(GENHLTH == "Good", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_fair = mean(GENHLTH == "Fair", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_poor = mean(GENHLTH == "Poor", na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_menthlth = mean(MENTHLTH, na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_employed=mean(EMPLOY1 == "Employed", na.rm=TRUE),
    prop_retired=mean(EMPLOY1 == "Retired", na.rm=TRUE),
    prop_self_employed=mean(EMPLOY1 == "Self-employed", na.rm=TRUE),
    prop_unable=mean(EMPLOY1 == "Unable to work", na.rm=TRUE),
    prop_homemaker=mean(EMPLOY1 == "Homemaker", na.rm=TRUE),
    prop_student=mean(EMPLOY1 == "Student", na.rm=TRUE),
    prop_democrat=mean(political_leaning == "Democrat", na.rm=TRUE),
    prop_republican=mean(political_leaning == "Republican", na.rm=TRUE),
    prop_alt1_strict = mean(alt_treatment1 == "Strict", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_alt1_not_strict = mean(alt_treatment1 == "Not Strict", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_alt2_strict = mean(alt_treatment2 == "Strict", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_alt2_not_strict = mean(alt_treatment2 == "Not Strict", na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

print(result4, width = Inf)

# Check whether missing race is related to other variables
result5 <- df %>%
  mutate(missing_age = is.na(`_AGEG5YR`)) %>%
  group_by(missing_age) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean_income = mean(as.numeric(INCOME2), na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_deaths = mean(deaths_per_100k, na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_male = mean(SEX == "Male", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_female = mean(SEX == "Female", na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_year=mean(as.numeric(Year), na.rm=TRUE),
    mean_menthlth = mean(MENTHLTH, na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_excellent = mean(GENHLTH == "Excellent", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_verygood = mean(GENHLTH == "Very good", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_good = mean(GENHLTH == "Good", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_fair = mean(GENHLTH == "Fair", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_poor = mean(GENHLTH == "Poor", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_white = mean(`_RACE` == "White", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_black = mean(`_RACE` == "Black", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_hispanic = mean(`_RACE` == "Hispanic", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_asian = mean(`_RACE` == "Asian", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_multiracial = mean(`_RACE` == "Multiracial", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_race_other = mean(`_RACE` == "Other", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_employed=mean(EMPLOY1 == "Employed", na.rm=TRUE),
    prop_retired=mean(EMPLOY1 == "Retired", na.rm=TRUE),
    prop_self_employed=mean(EMPLOY1 == "Self-employed", na.rm=TRUE),
    prop_unable=mean(EMPLOY1 == "Unable to work", na.rm=TRUE),
    prop_homemaker=mean(EMPLOY1 == "Homemaker", na.rm=TRUE),
    prop_student=mean(EMPLOY1 == "Student", na.rm=TRUE),
    mean_strict=mean(main_treatment=="Strict", na.rm=TRUE),
    mean_not_strict=mean(main_treatment=="Not Strict", na.rm=TRUE),
    prop_democrat=mean(political_leaning == "Democrat", na.rm=TRUE),
    prop_republican=mean(political_leaning == "Republican", na.rm=TRUE),
    prop_alt1_strict = mean(alt_treatment1 == "Strict", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_alt1_not_strict = mean(alt_treatment1 == "Not Strict", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_alt2_strict = mean(alt_treatment2 == "Strict", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_alt2_not_strict = mean(alt_treatment2 == "Not Strict", na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

print(result5, width = Inf)

# Check whether missing general health is related to other variables
result6 <- df %>%
  mutate(missing_genhlth = is.na(GENHLTH)) %>%
  group_by(missing_genhlth) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean_income = mean(as.numeric(INCOME2), na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_deaths = mean(deaths_per_100k, na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_male = mean(SEX == "Male", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_female = mean(SEX == "Female", na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_age_group = mean(as.numeric(`_AGEG5YR`), na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_year=mean(as.numeric(Year), na.rm=TRUE),
    mean_menthlth = mean(MENTHLTH, na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_white = mean(`_RACE` == "White", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_black = mean(`_RACE` == "Black", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_hispanic = mean(`_RACE` == "Hispanic", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_asian = mean(`_RACE` == "Asian", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_multiracial = mean(`_RACE` == "Multiracial", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_race_other = mean(`_RACE` == "Other", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_employed=mean(EMPLOY1 == "Employed", na.rm=TRUE),
    prop_retired=mean(EMPLOY1 == "Retired", na.rm=TRUE),
    prop_self_employed=mean(EMPLOY1 == "Self-employed", na.rm=TRUE),
    prop_unable=mean(EMPLOY1 == "Unable to work", na.rm=TRUE),
    prop_homemaker=mean(EMPLOY1 == "Homemaker", na.rm=TRUE),
    prop_student=mean(EMPLOY1 == "Student", na.rm=TRUE),
    mean_strict=mean(main_treatment=="Strict", na.rm=TRUE),
    mean_not_strict=mean(main_treatment=="Not Strict", na.rm=TRUE),
    prop_democrat=mean(political_leaning == "Democrat", na.rm=TRUE),
    prop_republican=mean(political_leaning == "Republican", na.rm=TRUE),
    prop_alt1_strict = mean(alt_treatment1 == "Strict", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_alt1_not_strict = mean(alt_treatment1 == "Not Strict", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_alt2_strict = mean(alt_treatment2 == "Strict", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_alt2_not_strict = mean(alt_treatment2 == "Not Strict", na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

print(result6, width = Inf)

# Check whether missing employment status is related to other variables
result7 <- df %>%
  mutate(missing_employ = is.na(EMPLOY1)) %>%
  group_by(missing_employ) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean_income = mean(as.numeric(INCOME2), na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_deaths = mean(deaths_per_100k, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_menthlth = mean(MENTHLTH, na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_male = mean(SEX == "Male", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_female = mean(SEX == "Female", na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_age_group = mean(as.numeric(`_AGEG5YR`), na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_year=mean(as.numeric(Year), na.rm=TRUE),
    mean_strict=mean(main_treatment=="Strict", na.rm=TRUE),
    mean_not_strict=mean(main_treatment=="Not Strict", na.rm=TRUE),
    prop_excellent = mean(GENHLTH == "Excellent", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_verygood = mean(GENHLTH == "Very good", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_good = mean(GENHLTH == "Good", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_fair = mean(GENHLTH == "Fair", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_poor = mean(GENHLTH == "Poor", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_white = mean(`_RACE` == "White", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_black = mean(`_RACE` == "Black", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_hispanic = mean(`_RACE` == "Hispanic", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_asian = mean(`_RACE` == "Asian", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_multiracial = mean(`_RACE` == "Multiracial", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_race_other = mean(`_RACE` == "Other", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_democrat=mean(political_leaning == "Democrat", na.rm=TRUE),
    prop_republican=mean(political_leaning == "Republican", na.rm=TRUE),
    prop_alt1_strict = mean(alt_treatment1 == "Strict", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_alt1_not_strict = mean(alt_treatment1 == "Not Strict", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_alt2_strict = mean(alt_treatment2 == "Strict", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_alt2_not_strict = mean(alt_treatment2 == "Not Strict", na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
print(result7, width = Inf)
```

```{r Handle Missing Values}
# remove observations with any missing values in the variables of interest
df_cc <- df %>%
  drop_na(MENTHLTH, SEX, `_RACE`, INCOME2, EMPLOY1, GENHLTH, `_AGEG5YR`, `_STATE`, Year, political_leaning, main_treatment, deaths_per_100k)

# check that no missing values remain in the cleaned dataset
df_cc %>% summarise(across(everything(), ~sum(is.na(.)))) |> 
  print(width = Inf)

# get number of observations after removing missing data
n_complete <- nrow(df_cc)

n_complete

# calculate how many observations were removed
n_removed <- n_original - n_complete
n_removed

# calculate percentage of observations removed due to missing data
pct_removed <- (n_removed / n_original) * 100
pct_removed

#Check that year distribution didn't change much with complete cases
before <- df |> summarize(.by = Year, count_before = n()) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(percent_before = count_before / sum(count_before) * 100)
after <- df_cc |> summarize(.by = Year, count_after = n()) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(percent_after = count_after / sum(count_after) * 100)
merge(before, after, .by = Year) |>
  mutate(percent_difference = abs(1 - (percent_after / percent_before)) * 100)
#some changes, but nothing drastic. All less than 5% different distribution and sufficient counts after to continue forward with the analysis
```

```{r Data Cleaning for DID Model to Run}
df_cc <- df_cc |> mutate(INCOME2 = fct_collapse(INCOME2,
                                                "$0-$25,000" = c("<$10,000",
                                                                 "$10,000-$15,000",
                                                                 "$15,000-$20,000",
                                                                 "$20,000-$25,000"),
                                                "$25,000-$50,000" =c("$25,000-$35,000",
                                                                    "$35,000-$50,000"),
                                                "$50,000+" = c("$50,000-$75,000",
                                                               "$75,000+")),
                         EMPLOY1 = fct_collapse(EMPLOY1,
                                                Employed = c("Employed",
                                                             "Self-employed"),
                                                Retired = "Retired",
                                                Unable = "Unable to work",
                                                NotWorking = c("Out of work <1 year", 
                                                               "Out of work 1+ years"),
                                                Other = c("Student", "Homemaker")),
                        `_RACE` = fct_collapse(`_RACE`,
                                               White = "White",
                                               Black = "Black",
                                               Hispanic = "Hispanic",
                                               Other = c("Asian", 
                                                         "American Indian/Alaskan Native","Other", "Multiracial", "Native Hawaiian/Pacific Islander")),
                        `_AGEG5YR` = fct_collapse(`_AGEG5YR`,
                                                  "18-34" = c("18-24","25-29",
                                                              "30-34"),
                                                  "35-49" = c("35-39","40-44", 
                                                              "45-49"),
                                                  "50-64" = c("50-54","55-59",
                                                              "60-64"),
                                                  "65+"   = c("65-69", "70-74",
                                                              "75-79", "80+"))) |>
  mutate(id = row_number(),
         MENTHLTH = as.numeric(MENTHLTH),
         Year = as.numeric(Year),
         political_leaning = as.factor(political_leaning)) |>
  mutate(Year = case_when(Year == 1 ~ 2017,
                          Year == 2 ~ 2018,
                          Year == 3 ~ 2019,
                          Year == 4 ~ 2020,
                          Year == 5 ~ 2021,
                          Year == 6 ~ 2022))
```

```{r DID Model for Main Treatment}
#Covariate check among treated vs control
df_cc |> select(main_treatment, SEX, `_RACE`, INCOME2, EMPLOY1, GENHLTH, 
                `_AGEG5YR`, political_leaning, deaths_per_100k) |>
  tbl_summary(by = main_treatment)
#Relatively equal distribution across strict/not strict for sex, race, income, employment, general health, age, and covid severity.
#Differences in political leaning, though that makes sense.

#Baseline DID analysis
main_model_data <- df_cc |> mutate(treatment_year = ifelse(main_treatment == "Strict", 2020, 0))
main_model_base <- att_gt(
  yname = "MENTHLTH",
  tname = "Year",
  idname = "id",
  gname = "treatment_year",
  xformla = ~ SEX + `_RACE` + INCOME2 + EMPLOY1 + GENHLTH + `_AGEG5YR` + 
    political_leaning + deaths_per_100k,
  panel = FALSE,
  data = main_model_data)
summary(main_model_base)
#No strong evidence that COVID strictness had significant impact on Mental Health
#Only significant difference is in 2018
#Parallel trends assumption violation (p = 0.00435)

#Visualize Results
ggdid(main_model_base)

#Overall ATT:
overall <- aggte(main_model_base, type = "simple")
summary(overall)
#Overall, not significant difference among treatment and control groups

#Parallel Trends Assumption
#Null hypothesis for summary: pre-trends are the same (want insignificant value)
#Violation of parallel trends assumption (p = 0.02669)
#Note: this visualization is NOT conditioned on covariates, they are raw outputs
main_model_data |>
  group_by(Year, main_treatment) |>
  summarize(avg_mental_health = mean(MENTHLTH, na.rm = TRUE)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = Year, y = avg_mental_health, color = main_treatment)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 2019.5, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(title = "Mental Health Trends by COVID Policy Strictness",
       x = "Year",
       y = "Average Poor Mental Health Days Per Month",
       color = "COVID Policy") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r Treatment Effects Heterogeneity: main_treatment}
#Sex
#Males
male_df <- main_model_data |> filter(SEX == "Male")
male_model <- att_gt(
  yname = "MENTHLTH",
  tname = "Year",
  idname = "id",
  gname = "treatment_year",
  xformla = ~ `_RACE` + INCOME2 + EMPLOY1 + GENHLTH + `_AGEG5YR` + 
    political_leaning + deaths_per_100k,
  panel = FALSE,
  data = male_df)
summary(male_model)
#No strong evidence that COVID strictness had significant impact on Mental Health in males.
#No parallel trends assumption violation (p = 0.413)

#Visualize Results
ggdid(male_model)

#Overall ATT:
overall <- aggte(male_model, type = "simple")
summary(overall)
#Overall, not significant difference among treatment and control groups for males

#Parallel Trends Assumption
#Null hypothesis for summary: pre-trends are the same (want insignificant value)
#No violation of parallel trends assumption (p = 0.413)
#Note: this visualization is NOT conditioned on covariates, they are raw outputs
male_df |>
  group_by(Year, main_treatment) |>
  summarize(avg_mental_health = mean(MENTHLTH, na.rm = TRUE)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = Year, y = avg_mental_health, color = main_treatment)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 2019.5, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(title = "Mental Health Trends by COVID Policy Strictness for Males",
       x = "Year",
       y = "Average Poor Mental Health Days Per Month",
       color = "COVID Policy") +
  theme_minimal()
#Females
female_df <- main_model_data |> filter(SEX == "Female")
female_model <- att_gt(
  yname = "MENTHLTH",
  tname = "Year",
  idname = "id",
  gname = "treatment_year",
  xformla = ~ `_RACE` + INCOME2 + EMPLOY1 + GENHLTH + `_AGEG5YR` + 
    political_leaning + deaths_per_100k,
  panel = FALSE,
  data = female_df)
summary(female_model)
#No strong evidence that COVID strictness had significant impact on Mental Health for females
#Only significant difference is in 2018
#Parallel trends assumption violation (p = 0.0071)

#Visualize Results
ggdid(female_model)

#Overall ATT:
overall <- aggte(female_model, type = "simple")
summary(overall)
#Overall, not significant difference among treatment and control groups for females

#Parallel Trends Assumption
#Null hypothesis for summary: pre-trends are the same (want insignificant value)
#Violation of parallel trends assumption (p = 0.0071)
#Note: this visualization is NOT conditioned on covariates, they are raw outputs
female_df |>
  group_by(Year, main_treatment) |>
  summarize(avg_mental_health = mean(MENTHLTH, na.rm = TRUE)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = Year, y = avg_mental_health, color = main_treatment)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 2019.5, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(title = "Mental Health Trends by COVID Policy Strictness for Females",
       x = "Year",
       y = "Average Poor Mental Health Days Per Month",
       color = "COVID Policy") +
  theme_minimal()

#Age
#Young adults
young_df <- main_model_data |> filter(`_AGEG5YR` == "18-34")
young_model <- att_gt(
  yname = "MENTHLTH",
  tname = "Year",
  idname = "id",
  gname = "treatment_year",
  xformla = ~ SEX + `_RACE` + INCOME2 + EMPLOY1 + GENHLTH + 
    political_leaning + deaths_per_100k,
  panel = FALSE,
  data = young_df)
summary(young_model)
#No strong evidence that COVID strictness had significant impact on Mental Health in young adults
#No parallel trends assumption violation (p = 0.05101)

#Visualize Results
ggdid(young_model)

#Overall ATT:
overall <- aggte(young_model, type = "simple")
summary(overall)
#Overall, not significant difference among treatment and control groups for young adults

#Parallel Trends Assumption
#Null hypothesis for summary: pre-trends are the same (want insignificant value)
#No violation of parallel trends assumption (p = 0.05101)
#Note: this visualization is NOT conditioned on covariates, they are raw outputs
young_df |>
  group_by(Year, main_treatment) |>
  summarize(avg_mental_health = mean(MENTHLTH, na.rm = TRUE)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = Year, y = avg_mental_health, color = main_treatment)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 2019.5, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(title = "Mental Health Trends by COVID Policy Strictness for Young Adults",
       x = "Year",
       y = "Average Poor Mental Health Days Per Month",
       color = "COVID Policy") +
  theme_minimal()
#Old
old_df <- main_model_data |> filter(`_AGEG5YR` != "18-34")
old_model <- att_gt(
  yname = "MENTHLTH",
  tname = "Year",
  idname = "id",
  gname = "treatment_year",
  xformla = ~ SEX + `_RACE` + INCOME2 + EMPLOY1 + GENHLTH + 
    political_leaning + deaths_per_100k,
  panel = FALSE,
  data = old_df)
summary(old_model)
#No strong evidence that COVID strictness had significant impact on Mental Health in people older than 35
#Parallel trends assumption violation (p = 0.01972)

#Visualize Results
ggdid(old_model)

#Overall ATT:
overall <- aggte(old_model, type = "simple")
summary(overall)
#Overall, not significant difference among treatment and control groups for 35+

#Parallel Trends Assumption
#Null hypothesis for summary: pre-trends are the same (want insignificant value)
#Violation of parallel trends assumption (p = 0.00077)
#Note: this visualization is NOT conditioned on covariates, they are raw outputs
old_df |>
  group_by(Year, main_treatment) |>
  summarize(avg_mental_health = mean(MENTHLTH, na.rm = TRUE)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = Year, y = avg_mental_health, color = main_treatment)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 2019.5, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(title = "Mental Health Trends by COVID Policy Strictness for 35+",
       x = "Year",
       y = "Average Poor Mental Health Days Per Month",
       color = "COVID Policy") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r DID Model for alt treatment 1}

# Compare covariates between treatment groups
df_cc |> 
  select(alt_treatment1, SEX, `_RACE`, INCOME2, EMPLOY1, GENHLTH, 
         `_AGEG5YR`, political_leaning, deaths_per_100k) |>
  tbl_summary(by = alt_treatment1)

# Create treatment timing variable (2020 = treated, 0 = control)
alt1_model_data <- df_cc |> 
  mutate(treatment_year = ifelse(alt_treatment1 == "Strict", 2020, 0))


# Estimate DID model using Callaway and Sant’Anna method
alt1_model <- att_gt(
  yname = "MENTHLTH",                     
  tname = "Year",                      
  idname = "id",                          
  gname = "treatment_year",              
  xformla = ~ SEX + `_RACE` + INCOME2 + EMPLOY1 + GENHLTH + `_AGEG5YR` + 
    political_leaning + deaths_per_100k,   
  panel = FALSE,                          
  data = alt1_model_data                       
)

# Display model results
summary(alt1_model)

# Plot group-time treatment effects
ggdid(alt1_model)

# Calculate overall average treatment effect
overall_alt1 <- aggte(alt1_model, type = "simple")
summary(overall_alt1)

# Estimate event-study (dynamic) treatment effects
dynamic_alt1 <- aggte(alt1_model, type = "dynamic")
summary(dynamic_alt1)

# Plot average mental health trends by treatment group
alt1_model_data |>
  group_by(Year, alt_treatment1) |>
  summarize(avg_mental_health = mean(MENTHLTH, na.rm = TRUE)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = Year, y = avg_mental_health, color = alt_treatment1)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 2019.5, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(title = "Mental Health Trends by COVID Policy Strictness",
       x = "Year",
       y = "Average Poor Mental Health Days Per Month",
       color = "COVID Policy") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r DID for Alt treatment 2}
df_cc |> select(alt_treatment2, SEX, `_RACE`, INCOME2, EMPLOY1, GENHLTH, 
                `_AGEG5YR`, political_leaning, deaths_per_100k) |>
  tbl_summary(by = alt_treatment2)

#Baseline DID analysis
alt2_model_data <- df_cc |> mutate(treatment_year = ifelse(alt_treatment2 == "Strict", 2020, 0))

alt2_model_base <- att_gt(
  yname = "MENTHLTH",
  tname = "Year",
  idname = "id",
  gname = "treatment_year",
  xformla = ~ SEX + `_RACE` + INCOME2 + EMPLOY1 + GENHLTH + `_AGEG5YR` + 
    political_leaning + deaths_per_100k,
  panel = FALSE,
  data = alt2_model_data) 

summary(alt2_model_base)
#No strong evidence that COVID strictness had significant impact on Mental Health
#Only significant difference is in 2018
#Parallel trends assumption violation (p = 0.00435)

#Visualize Results
ggdid(alt2_model_base)

#Overall ATT:
overall_alt2 <- aggte(alt2_model_base, type = "simple")
summary(overall_alt2)
#Overall, not significant difference among treatment and control groups

#Parallel Trends Assumption
#Null hypothesis for summary: pre-trends are the same (want insignificant value)
#Violation of parallel trends assumption (p = 0.02669)
#Note: this visualization is NOT conditioned on covariates, they are raw outputs
main_model_data |>
  group_by(Year, alt_treatment2) |>
  summarize(avg_mental_health = mean(MENTHLTH, na.rm = TRUE)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = Year, y = avg_mental_health, color = alt_treatment2)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 2019.5, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(title = "Mental Health Trends by COVID Severity",
       x = "Year",
       y = "Average Poor Mental Health Days Per Month",
       color = "COVID Policy") +
  theme_minimal()
```
